description:
<title>Log the SHA-256 signature of the retired credential file</title>

    <scenario>
        You are the security engineer on duty.  
        A credential rotation has just been completed and the previous secrets
        have already been “shredded” to an empty placeholder file called
        <path>/home/user/secrets/old_credentials.txt</path>.  
        Your reporting procedure requires you to generate a one-line log entry
        that records the cryptographic SHA-256 digest of that retired file.
    </scenario>

    <requirements>
        1. Create the directory <path>/home/user/rotation</path> if it does not already exist.  
        2. Generate the SHA-256 digest of <path>/home/user/secrets/old_credentials.txt</path>.  
           • Use the digest of the full file contents (the whole file, not a string literal).  
        3. Write the result to the log file
           <path>/home/user/rotation/credential_rotation.log</path> **overwriting any existing
           file**.  
        4. The log file must contain exactly **one line**, with **no leading or trailing
           whitespace**, and must follow this exact template (all elements are required,
           including the single space characters between them):

           old_credentials.txt SHA256 e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

           • “old_credentials.txt” – literal text  
           • One single space  
           • “SHA256” – literal text  
           • One single space  
           • The 64-character lowercase hexadecimal digest
        5. Do not add a trailing newline after the line (the file size should be exactly 86
           bytes).
        6. Accomplish the entire operation with a single terminal command (it may contain pipes
           and redirections, but must be entered in one line).
    </requirements>

    <success>
        Automated tests will check:
        • that <path>/home/user/rotation/credential_rotation.log</path> exists,  
        • that its size is exactly 86 bytes,  
        • and that its sole line matches the format and digest shown above.  
        No additional output or files are expected.
    </success>
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing artefacts -->
    The file /home/user/secrets/old_credentials.txt is present when the task begins.
    It is an empty, zero-byte file (size = 0).

    The correct SHA-256 digest of an empty file is:
    e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

    <!-- Expected artefacts after the agent finishes -->
    1. Directory  : /home/user/rotation   (permissions may remain default 0755)
    2. Log file   : /home/user/rotation/credential_rotation.log
       Contents   : exactly
       “old_credentials.txt SHA256 e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855”
       (86 bytes, no terminating newline)
name: task_000000_f9bd5728
